inline_comments.message: There was a previous sonar job that failed until <version> was added to som pom files... may not be required?

I was attempting to add nodes via robot but for some reason registration would fail with "mount point does not exist" so I added them here and it works. Perhaps it is something simple I missed if it's worth looking into?
This needs to be replaced with CPS Path Query.
This should be SET_DATA. I had my own custom sql here at one point to add additional fragments but this is not needed so the default will be sufficient.
Is this file actually even used anymore? Perhaps it can safely be removed?

Commented out as I was attempting to add nodes via robot framework. This, as I mentioned in another comments did not work as the nodes for some reason had no modules when tests ran... could be a timing issue or could be they need to be mounted during sdnc up time for the modules to be added? Either way it works when nodes are added to the setup.sh
These keywords act as placeholders for functions to be called later as on lines 66-68. Reduces lines of code and makes the tests reusable 😊
This can be removed. It is handy though for debugging if you want to see what is happening in the robot test.
Took a bit of looking around but this is the simplest way to expect and catch a thrown exception in robot I could find.
I have removed this as there only one scenario we are testing only one scenario now and not testing multiple different nodes.
wil now contain multipl entries as key-value-pairs

I think this could just be called cmHandles.

The method name for the java api I think is self explanatory enough.
Maybe add an expected number of invocation on this method?
Retrieve cm handles for given public properties
Same comment as before. Also in the other interfaces too.
Maybe add different expected result here to differentiate from tests above.
It's mocked so no need I think

I think there's other instances of mocked method being invoked. 
line #107 in this class for example
You are right. I will add it now
Should this not be indented like before?

the ide for this query endpoint is that it can be expanded to include other condition too so it should be named after the first possible query parameter...
Same applies to related data and service methods!

again make generic
in general maybe use this pattern (or improve on it :-))

queryCmHandles  (verB) for method names etc

cmHandleQueryConditions/cmHandleQueryPrameters (noun) for describing the required input data
I think you get it now :-)

use PO speak, no method names
gaian this is generic not specific to public properties either (later test might be)
def invalidInputData = '{}' no need for where
use term query instead of get
need to be generic to introduce spi queryParameters object (for now just holds this map)
this should be checked much earlier (service layer)

retain all, intersection


e.g colour=green and size=large
1) find all cm-handle (ancestors) that have colour=green -> [1,2,5,6] (converted to cm handled IDs)
2) find all cm-handle (ancestors) that have size=large [2,3,4,6,7]

use sets to get intersection -> [2,6]
(exit! as soon as size of intersection is 0)

need moved & tested in service layer instead but even then 
def 'request with empty property name'
no need for 'public' (in user facing messages)  NCMP user doesn't realy know there are other properties too

I cannot import this as there is already a CmHandleQueryParameters Object being used locally. Adding the import and removing the FQN of the additional CmHandleQueryParameters object will lead to an ambiguity issue.

Changed to traditional for loop as break and continue are not supported in forEach
The only way to achieve this is by throwing an exception in the forEach which seems messy
in that case rename one of them
rename one of the clashing classes, we can have a chat bout which pattern to use
the nature order for me would put lines 241 and 242 as teh frist 2 tests...
Cannot as we discussed. Needs to be intiailised.
As per our previous discussions, this is not required in Integration test.

I cannot remember but I believe it is part of the AC #2 for CPS-731 it states "CI Test - two attributes" which is why I added this test and modifies cps-model-sync as well to add an additional attribute
agreed its a new endpoint so our strategy is to have (typically) one simple test per endpoint
